norwegian: Forskningsresultatene tyder på at sammenhengen mellom søvn og hukommelse er sterkere enn tidligere antatt.
english: The research results suggest that the connection between sleep and memory is stronger than previously assumed.
notes: Academic hedging: 'tyder på at' (suggests that)

norwegian: Det kan argumenteres for at klimapolitikken ikke har hatt tilstrekkelig effekt på utslippsnivåene.
english: It can be argued that climate policy has not had a sufficient effect on emission levels.
notes: Impersonal argumentative construction: 'det kan argumenteres for at'

norwegian: Ifølge flere studier _____ digitale hjelpemidler læringsutbyttet i grunnskolen.
english: According to several studies, digital tools improve learning outcomes in primary school.
notes: forbedrer — V2 rule after fronted adverbial 'ifølge flere studier'

norwegian: Det er verdt å merke seg at resultatene fra denne undersøkelsen ikke uten videre kan generaliseres.
english: It is worth noting that the results from this survey cannot straightforwardly be generalized.
notes: Hedging phrase: 'ikke uten videre' (not straightforwardly/automatically)

norwegian: Som nevnt i innledningen, bygger denne analysen på et begrenset datamateriale.
english: As mentioned in the introduction, this analysis is based on a limited data set.
notes: Intertextual reference phrase; V2 after fronted adverbial clause

norwegian: Funnene i denne rapporten _____ tidligere forskning på feltet.
english: The findings in this report support earlier research in the field.
notes: støtter — academic verb for supporting prior research

norwegian: Det foreligger lite forskning på hvordan sosiale medier påvirker politisk deltakelse blant unge voksne.
english: There is little research on how social media affects political participation among young adults.
notes: 'det foreligger' is a formal existential construction meaning 'there exists/there is available'

norwegian: Hensikten med denne artikkelen er å belyse de viktigste utfordringene knyttet til digital personvern.
english: The purpose of this article is to shed light on the most important challenges related to digital privacy.
notes: 'hensikten med ... er å' (the purpose of ... is to) — formal purpose framing

norwegian: Det er rimelig å anta at befolkningsveksten vil fortsette å legge press på boligmarkedet.
english: It is reasonable to assume that population growth will continue to put pressure on the housing market.
notes: 'det er rimelig å anta at' — formal hedging, reasonable assumption marker

norwegian: Teorien _____ ikke av alle forskere, og det finnes alternative forklaringsmodeller.
english: The theory is not accepted by all researchers, and there are alternative explanatory models.
notes: aksepteres — passive present tense in academic context

norwegian: På bakgrunn av dette kan vi ikke trekke noen endelige konklusjoner uten ytterligere undersøkelser.
english: On this basis, we cannot draw any definitive conclusions without further investigation.
notes: V2 after 'på bakgrunn av dette'; formal hedging on conclusions

norwegian: Studien antyder at det er en signifikant korrelasjon mellom stress og søvnkvalitet hos studenter.
english: The study suggests that there is a significant correlation between stress and sleep quality in students.
notes: 'antyder at' (suggests that) — softer claim verb than 'viser'

norwegian: I lys av disse funnene _____ det nødvendig å revidere den eksisterende politikken.
english: In light of these findings, it seems necessary to revise the existing policy.
notes: synes — V2 after fronted 'i lys av disse funnene'

norwegian: Denne tilnærmingen har vist seg å være mer effektiv enn tradisjonelle metoder i flere kontrollerte forsøk.
english: This approach has proven to be more effective than traditional methods in several controlled trials.
notes: 'har vist seg å være' — reflexive evidential construction for proven results

norwegian: Det gjenstår å se om disse tiltakene vil ha den ønskede effekten på lang sikt.
english: It remains to be seen whether these measures will have the desired effect in the long term.
notes: 'det gjenstår å se om' — formal expression of uncertainty about future outcomes

norwegian: Resultatene er i tråd med hypotesen om at økt fysisk aktivitet reduserer symptomer på depresjon.
english: The results are in line with the hypothesis that increased physical activity reduces symptoms of depression.
notes: 'i tråd med' (in line with) — formal alignment phrase

norwegian: Det bør understrekes at utvalget i denne studien ikke er representativt for hele befolkningen.
english: It should be emphasised that the sample in this study is not representative of the entire population.
notes: 'det bør understrekes at' — formal caveat phrase

norwegian: Metoden som ble benyttet, _____ i detalj i metodedelen av rapporten.
english: The method that was used is described in detail in the methodology section of the report.
notes: beskrives — passive present tense in academic writing

norwegian: Denne studien bidrar til å fylle et gap i litteraturen ved å undersøke faktorer som tidligere er oversett.
english: This study helps to fill a gap in the literature by examining factors that have previously been overlooked.
notes: 'bidrar til å fylle et gap i litteraturen' — standard academic contribution framing

norwegian: Basert på de foreliggende dataene _____ det vanskelig å fastslå årsaksforholdet med sikkerhet.
english: Based on the available data, it is difficult to establish the causal relationship with certainty.
notes: er — V2 inversion; 'basert på' fronted prepositional phrase triggers inversion

norwegian: Selv om dataene er begrenset, gir de likevel et nyttig innblikk i fenomenet.
english: Although the data is limited, it still provides a useful insight into the phenomenon.
notes: V2 after 'selv om'-clause; 'likevel' as concessive adverb in main clause

norwegian: Det er all grunn til å tro at videre forskning vil avdekke nye aspekter ved dette spørsmålet.
english: There is every reason to believe that further research will uncover new aspects of this question.
notes: 'det er all grunn til å tro at' — strong academic hedging phrase

norwegian: Analysen avslører et komplekst mønster av faktorer som samvirker på uforutsigbare måter.
english: The analysis reveals a complex pattern of factors that interact in unpredictable ways.
notes: 'samvirker' (interact/work together) — formal academic verb

norwegian: Funnene _____ at en mer helhetlig tilnærming er nødvendig for å løse problemet effektivt.
english: The findings indicate that a more holistic approach is necessary to solve the problem effectively.
notes: indikerer — formal reporting verb

norwegian: Spørsmålet om hvorvidt teknologi kan erstatte menneskelig kontakt er fortsatt omdiskutert.
english: The question of whether technology can replace human contact remains contested.
notes: 'hvorvidt' (whether) — formal conjunction used in academic writing

norwegian: Denne artikkelen tar sikte på å gi en oversikt over de viktigste teoriene innen feltet.
english: This article aims to provide an overview of the most important theories within the field.
notes: 'tar sikte på å' (aims to) — formal purpose verb phrase

norwegian: I motsetning til tidligere studier _____ denne undersøkelsen også de langsiktige konsekvensene.
english: In contrast to earlier studies, this investigation also examines the long-term consequences.
notes: undersøker — V2 inversion after fronted 'i motsetning til'

norwegian: Det kan hevdes at den eksisterende lovgivningen ikke er tilpasset de nye teknologiske realitetene.
english: It can be maintained that the existing legislation is not adapted to the new technological realities.
notes: 'det kan hevdes at' — impersonal argumentative construction

norwegian: Disse funnene reiser viktige spørsmål om etikken rundt bruk av kunstig intelligens i helsesektoren.
english: These findings raise important questions about the ethics of using artificial intelligence in the health sector.
notes: 'reiser viktige spørsmål om' — academic framing for raising issues

norwegian: Konklusjonen er at ytterligere empirisk forskning er påkrevd før politiske tiltak kan iverksettes.
english: The conclusion is that further empirical research is required before policy measures can be implemented.
notes: 'er påkrevd' — formal passive expressing necessity; 'iverksettes' (implemented) — formal passive infinitive

norwegian: Kunne De være så vennlig å sende meg den aktuelle rapporten så snart det lar seg gjøre?
english: Could you be so kind as to send me the relevant report as soon as possible?
notes: Formal 'De/Dem' address; 'så snart det lar seg gjøre' = as soon as it can be arranged

norwegian: Hei! Skal vi ikke bare ta en kaffe og prate litt? Det er lenge siden sist.
english: Hey! Shall we not just grab a coffee and chat a bit? It's been a long time.
notes: Informal register: 'Hei', 'bare', 'prate litt', 'lenge siden sist' — all informal markers

norwegian: Jeg tillater meg å henvende meg til Dem angående den forestående kontraktsforhandlingen.
english: I take the liberty of contacting you regarding the upcoming contract negotiation.
notes: 'tillater meg å henvende meg' — very formal self-deprecating introductory phrase; 'Dem' formal address

norwegian: I møtet i dag _____ det diskutert flere forslag til effektivisering av arbeidsprosessene.
english: In today's meeting, several proposals for streamlining work processes were discussed.
notes: ble — passive past tense; formal workplace register

norwegian: Vet du hva, jeg skjønte egentlig ikke hva han mente, men jeg lot som om jeg gjorde.
english: You know what, I didn't really understand what he meant, but I pretended I did.
notes: Informal: 'vet du hva', 'egentlig', 'lot som om' — conversational filler and expression

norwegian: Vi ber om unnskyldning for eventuelle ulemper dette måtte medføre.
english: We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.
notes: 'ber om unnskyldning for' — formal apology; 'eventuelle' = any/possible (formal hedging adjective)

norwegian: Herr direktør, _____ jeg få lov til å presentere våre foreløpige resultater?
english: Mr. Director, may I have permission to present our preliminary results?
notes: måtte/ville — 'måtte jeg få lov til' is the highly formal request form

norwegian: Det er utrolig kult at du fikk den jobben! Når begynner du?
english: It's incredibly cool that you got that job! When do you start?
notes: Informal: 'utrolig kult' — would be completely inappropriate in formal context

norwegian: Selskapet ønsker å uttrykke sin dypeste takknemlighet for Deres mangeårige og trofaste tjeneste.
english: The company wishes to express its deepest gratitude for your many years of faithful service.
notes: 'Deres' — formal possessive; 'trofaste tjeneste' — formal, somewhat archaic

norwegian: Æsj, den filmen var jo helt håpløs. Vi burde ha valgt noe annet.
english: Ugh, that film was absolutely hopeless. We should have chosen something else.
notes: 'Æsj' — informal exclamation; 'jo' — informal confirmation particle; 'håpløs' colloquially meaning terrible

norwegian: Med henvisning til Deres brev av 15. mai ønsker vi å klargjøre vår posisjon i saken.
english: With reference to your letter of 15 May, we wish to clarify our position in the matter.
notes: 'Med henvisning til' — standard formal business letter opening phrase

norwegian: Fikk du sett den kampen i går? Den var helt rå, altså!
english: Did you catch that match yesterday? It was absolutely brilliant!
notes: 'rå' (raw/brutal) — informal slang for impressive; 'altså' — informal emphasis particle

norwegian: Styret _____ enstemmig vedtatt å utsette avgjørelsen til neste kvartal.
english: The board has unanimously decided to postpone the decision until next quarter.
notes: har — present perfect; formal board/corporate register

norwegian: Jeg har ikke peiling på hva som foregår. Kan du ikke bare forklare det ordentlig?
english: I have no idea what's going on. Can you not just explain it properly?
notes: 'ikke peiling' — informal idiom for having no idea; 'bare' and 'ordentlig' are informal intensifiers

norwegian: Undertegnede ønsker herved å søke om permisjon fra sin stilling i perioden 1.–30. juni.
english: The undersigned hereby wishes to apply for leave of absence from their position during the period 1–30 June.
notes: 'Undertegnede' — formal self-reference (the undersigned); 'herved' — formal adverb (hereby)

norwegian: Gitt selskapets finansielle situasjon _____ det nødvendig å foreta umiddelbare tiltak.
english: Given the company's financial situation, it is necessary to take immediate action.
notes: er — V2 inversion after fronted participial phrase 'gitt selskapets finansielle situasjon'

norwegian: Det var ikke meningen å støte deg, men jeg synes ærlig talt at du overreagerte litt.
english: It wasn't meant to offend you, but honestly I think you overreacted a little.
notes: Semi-formal/neutral spoken register; 'ærlig talt' (honestly speaking) and 'litt' soften the criticism

norwegian: Vi ser frem til et fortsatt godt samarbeid og ønsker Dem alt godt i Deres nye stilling.
english: We look forward to a continued good working relationship and wish you all the best in your new position.
notes: Formal closing phrase; 'Dem'/'Deres' — formal address throughout

norwegian: Du, jeg måtte bare si at du virkelig er flink til å håndtere slike situasjoner.
english: Hey, I just had to say that you are really good at handling those kinds of situations.
notes: Conversational 'du' as attention-getter; 'bare' as informal softener; 'flink til' — informal praise

norwegian: Departementet _____ at søknadene vil bli behandlet innen to uker fra mottaksdato.
english: The ministry informs that applications will be processed within two weeks from the date of receipt.
notes: opplyser — formal verb for 'informs/states'; bureaucratic register

norwegian: Sjefen min sa «god jobb» til meg i dag. Ikke dårlig med tanke på at det har vært en tøff uke.
english: My boss said 'good job' to me today. Not bad considering it has been a tough week.
notes: Neutral/informal spoken register about a workplace context; contrast with formal workplace writing

norwegian: Vi gjør oppmerksom på at betalingsfristen for den aktuelle fakturaen nå er overskredet.
english: We would like to draw your attention to the fact that the payment deadline for the relevant invoice has now been exceeded.
notes: 'gjør oppmerksom på' — formal phrase for drawing attention; 'den aktuelle' = the relevant (formal adjective)

norwegian: Etter en grundig _____ av alternativene anbefaler komitéen å velge det andre forslaget.
english: After a thorough assessment of the alternatives, the committee recommends choosing the second proposal.
notes: vurdering — formal noun for assessment/evaluation

norwegian: Kom igjen, ta det med ro. Det ordner seg sikkert, du skal se.
english: Come on, take it easy. It'll probably work out, you'll see.
notes: Informal reassurance: 'kom igjen', 'ta det med ro', 'ordner seg' — all informal expressions

norwegian: Forespørselen Deres er mottatt og vil bli behandlet av ansvarlig saksbehandler.
english: Your enquiry has been received and will be processed by the responsible case handler.
notes: 'Forespørselen Deres' fronted; 'saksbehandler' — formal bureaucratic job title

norwegian: Vi setter stor pris på Deres _____ og gleder oss til å møte Dem personlig.
english: We greatly appreciate your patience and look forward to meeting you in person.
notes: tålmodighet — 'sette stor pris på' is formal appreciation; 'Deres/Dem' formal address

norwegian: Orker du ikke gidde å svare på meldingene mine? Det er litt ugreit.
english: Can't you be bothered to reply to my messages? That's a bit off.
notes: 'orker ikke gidde' — double low-energy informal verbs; 'ugreit' — informal for not OK/awkward

norwegian: Vedtaket ble fattet med seks stemmer mot to, og vil tre i kraft fra og med neste regnskapsår.
english: The decision was made with six votes to two, and will come into effect from the next financial year.
notes: 'vedtaket ble fattet' — formal passive; 'tre i kraft' (come into force) — formal legal phrase

norwegian: Takk for sist, forresten! Vi må gjøre det igjen snart.
english: Thanks for last time, by the way! We must do it again soon.
notes: 'takk for sist' — uniquely Norwegian social phrase thanking someone for a previous meeting

norwegian: Konferansen _____ avholdt på Radisson Blu Hotel, og samtlige presentasjoner vil bli strømmet direkte.
english: The conference will be held at Radisson Blu Hotel, and all presentations will be streamed live.
notes: vil bli — future passive; 'samtlige' = all/every (formal); 'avholdt' — formal passive past participle

norwegian: Bilen ble reparert av en erfaren mekaniker på verkstedet i nærheten.
english: The car was repaired by an experienced mechanic at the nearby workshop.
notes: 'ble reparert av' — agentive passive with 'av' + agent

norwegian: Rapporten er allerede levert, men tilbakemeldingene er ikke mottatt ennå.
english: The report has already been submitted, but the feedback has not been received yet.
notes: Stative passive: 'er levert' / 'er mottatt' — state resulting from action, not the action itself

norwegian: Døren er _____ fra innsiden, og ingen kan komme ut.
english: The door is locked from the inside, and no one can get out.
notes: låst — stative passive (er + past participle = resulting state)

norwegian: Det bygges flere nye leiligheter i sentrum for å møte den voksende etterspørselen.
english: Several new apartments are being built in the centre to meet growing demand.
notes: Impersonal s-passive: 'det bygges' — agentless, describes ongoing process

norwegian: Alle dokumentene ble gjennomgått nøye av revisorene før rapporten ble godkjent.
english: All the documents were carefully reviewed by the auditors before the report was approved.
notes: Two passive constructions in sequence; 'av revisorene' — agentive phrase

norwegian: Resultatene _____ offentliggjort i neste ukes avis.
english: The results will be made public in next week's newspaper.
notes: vil bli — future passive construction (vil bli + past participle)

norwegian: I løpet av de siste årene er det gjort store fremskritt innen behandling av kreft.
english: Over the last few years, great advances have been made in the treatment of cancer.
notes: Impersonal passive: 'er det gjort' — fronted time adverbial triggers V2 with det-subject

norwegian: Pakken ble sendt mandag og burde allerede _____ mottatt.
english: The package was sent on Monday and should already have been received.
notes: ha blitt — modal + perfect passive: 'burde ha blitt mottatt'

norwegian: Vinduene er satt inn, gulvet er lagt, og huset er nesten ferdig.
english: The windows have been installed, the floor has been laid, and the house is almost finished.
notes: Three stative passives in sequence; all describe a resulting state

norwegian: Det ble ikke tatt noen endelig beslutning i møtet, og saken utsettes til neste uke.
english: No final decision was taken in the meeting, and the matter is being postponed until next week.
notes: Impersonal passive: 'det ble tatt'; s-passive: 'utsettes' — both agentless

norwegian: Han ble bedt om å forlate møtet etter den høylytte diskusjonen.
english: He was asked to leave the meeting after the heated discussion.
notes: 'ble bedt om å' — passive with infinitive complement

norwegian: Boken er skrevet på en måte som gjør den tilgjengelig for et bredt publikum.
english: The book is written in a way that makes it accessible to a wide audience.
notes: Stative passive 'er skrevet' describes the book's condition/manner

norwegian: Det _____ ikke funnet noen feil i systemet etter den grundige gjennomgangen.
english: No faults were found in the system after the thorough review.
notes: ble — impersonal passive: 'det ble ikke funnet'

norwegian: Ny forskning viser at mange sykdommer kan forebygges dersom de oppdages tidlig nok.
english: New research shows that many diseases can be prevented if they are detected early enough.
notes: Modal s-passive: 'kan forebygges', 'oppdages' — agentless modal passives

norwegian: Prosjektet ble avsluttet i tide og er nå overlevert til kunden.
english: The project was completed on time and has now been handed over to the client.
notes: Dynamic passive 'ble avsluttet' vs. stative passive 'er overlevert' — contrast

norwegian: Alle ble informert om endringen, men ikke alle hadde _____ e-posten.
english: Everyone was informed of the change, but not everyone had read the email.
notes: lest — pluperfect active after passive in coordinated clause

norwegian: Det forventes at alle ansatte _____ for sertifiseringskurset innen utgangen av måneden.
english: It is expected that all employees register for the certification course by the end of the month.
notes: registrerer seg — impersonal passive 'det forventes at' + active clause

norwegian: Husene som ble bygget på 1970-tallet er nå i ferd med å renoveres.
english: The houses that were built in the 1970s are now in the process of being renovated.
notes: 'i ferd med å renoveres' — progressive passive construction

norwegian: Kontrakten må signeres av begge parter før noen arbeid kan påbegynnes.
english: The contract must be signed by both parties before any work can be commenced.
notes: Modal passive: 'må signeres av'; s-passive: 'kan påbegynnes'

norwegian: Feilen ble oppdaget av en tilfeldig bruker og _____ straks videreformidlet til utviklingsteamet.
english: The error was discovered by a random user and was immediately passed on to the development team.
notes: ble — coordinated passive: 'ble oppdaget ... og ble straks videreformidlet'

norwegian: Det antas at omtrent to millioner nordmenn er direkte berørt av de nye skattereglene.
english: It is assumed that approximately two million Norwegians are directly affected by the new tax rules.
notes: Impersonal passive 'det antas at'; stative passive 'er berørt'

norwegian: Systemet er designet slik at feil automatisk _____ og loggføres.
english: The system is designed so that errors are automatically detected and logged.
notes: oppdages — s-passive in coordinated clause: 'oppdages og loggføres'

norwegian: Landsbyen ble grunnlagt i middelalderen og er i dag et populært turistmål.
english: The village was founded in the Middle Ages and is today a popular tourist destination.
notes: Historical passive 'ble grunnlagt'; stative 'er' in present — temporal contrast

norwegian: Det sies at nordmenn er verdens lykkeligste folk, men det er ikke lett å måle lykke.
english: It is said that Norwegians are the world's happiest people, but it is not easy to measure happiness.
notes: S-passive 'det sies' — impersonal passive for common knowledge/hearsay

norwegian: Vedlikeholdsarbeidet _____ gjennomført i løpet av natten for å unngå forstyrrelser i arbeidstiden.
english: The maintenance work will be carried out during the night to avoid disruptions during working hours.
notes: vil bli — future passive: 'vil bli gjennomført'

norwegian: Samtlige forslag ble vurdert, men bare to av dem ble antatt til videre behandling.
english: All proposals were evaluated, but only two of them were accepted for further processing.
notes: Two passive clauses with 'samtlige' (formal for 'all') and agentless construction

norwegian: Loven ble vedtatt av Stortinget i 2019 og trådte i kraft fra 1. januar 2020.
english: The law was passed by the Storting in 2019 and came into force from 1 January 2020.
notes: 'ble vedtatt av' — agentive passive; 'trådte i kraft' — active idiom for coming into force

norwegian: Det er ikke avklart hvem som skal ha ansvaret for å dekke de ekstra kostnadene.
english: It has not been clarified who is to be responsible for covering the extra costs.
notes: Stative passive 'er ikke avklart'; impersonal 'det' subject

norwegian: Funnene ble presentert på konferansen og ble positivt mottatt av fagmiljøet.
english: The findings were presented at the conference and were positively received by the research community.
notes: Coordinated passives; 'positivt mottatt' — adverb modifying passive past participle

norwegian: Prisene på boliger har blitt drevet opp av lav rente og høy etterspørsel de siste årene.
english: Housing prices have been driven up by low interest rates and high demand in recent years.
notes: Present perfect passive: 'har blitt drevet opp av' — with agentive 'av'-phrase

norwegian: Selv om han hadde forberedt seg grundig, følte han seg ikke trygg rett før presentasjonen.
english: Even though he had prepared thoroughly, he did not feel confident just before the presentation.
notes: 'selv om' — concessive subordinator; V2 in main clause after subordinate

norwegian: Til tross for at budsjettet ble kuttet, klarte teamet likevel å levere prosjektet i tide.
english: Despite the budget being cut, the team still managed to deliver the project on time.
notes: 'til tross for at' — concessive; 'likevel' (nevertheless) — concessive adverb in main clause

norwegian: Mange støtter forslaget; _____ er det viktig å høre kritikerne også.
english: Many support the proposal; however, it is important to also listen to the critics.
notes: imidlertid — formal contrastive connector; note inversion when fronted

norwegian: Selv om teknologien finnes, er det ikke gitt at den vil bli tatt i bruk i stor skala.
english: Even though the technology exists, it is not guaranteed that it will be adopted on a large scale.
notes: 'ikke gitt at' — Norwegian idiom: not a given that; V2 after 'selv om'-clause

norwegian: Han er svært intelligent; _____ mangler han evnen til å samarbeide med andre.
english: He is very intelligent; nevertheless, he lacks the ability to collaborate with others.
notes: likevel — concessive adverb; note V2: 'likevel mangler han'

norwegian: Til tross for de mange utfordringene har regjeringen opprettholdt sin støtte til prosjektet.
english: Despite the many challenges, the government has maintained its support for the project.
notes: 'til tross for' + noun phrase (no 'at'); V2 inversion in main clause

norwegian: Forslaget er interessant; det er _____ for tidlig å si om det vil virke i praksis.
english: The proposal is interesting; it is, however, too early to say whether it will work in practice.
notes: imidlertid — parenthetical placement mid-clause; note comma usage

norwegian: Selv om mange er skeptiske, peker forskningen _____ på at dette er den riktige veien å gå.
english: Even though many are sceptical, the research nevertheless points to this being the right path.
notes: likevel — adverb after finite verb when not fronted: 'peker likevel på'

norwegian: På den ene siden tilbyr løsningen stor fleksibilitet; på den andre siden krever den mye ressurser.
english: On the one hand, the solution offers great flexibility; on the other hand, it requires a lot of resources.
notes: 'på den ene siden ... på den andre siden' — balanced contrastive structure with V2

norwegian: Til tross for at hun hadde fått avslag, ga hun ikke opp og søkte på nytt.
english: Despite having been rejected, she did not give up and applied again.
notes: 'til tross for at' + subordinate clause; 'ga ikke opp' — active idiomatic response

norwegian: Selv om prisen er høy, er kvaliteten _____ god nok til å rettferdiggjøre investeringen.
english: Even though the price is high, the quality is still good enough to justify the investment.
notes: likevel — concessive adverb mid-clause: 'er kvaliteten likevel god nok'

norwegian: Tiltaket er kostbart; det kan _____ ikke avvises uten videre analyse.
english: The measure is costly; it can, however, not be dismissed without further analysis.
notes: imidlertid — parenthetical between subject and negation: 'kan imidlertid ikke'

norwegian: Selv om det ikke er noen enkel løsning, må vi _____ gjøre et forsøk på å løse problemet.
english: Even though there is no simple solution, we must still make an attempt to solve the problem.
notes: likevel — 'må vi likevel gjøre'; inversion in main clause after 'selv om'

norwegian: Derimot viser statistikken at kriminaliteten faktisk har gått ned de siste ti årene.
english: On the contrary, the statistics show that crime has actually decreased over the last ten years.
notes: 'derimot' — contrastive marker meaning 'on the contrary'; V2 inversion

norwegian: Til tross for høye temperaturer valgte mange å bli i byene i stedet for å dra på ferie.
english: Despite the high temperatures, many chose to stay in the cities instead of going on holiday.
notes: 'til tross for' + noun phrase; V2 inversion; 'i stedet for å' (instead of + infinitive)

norwegian: Selv om det er fristende å akseptere det første tilbudet, lønner det seg å sammenligne alternativene.
english: Even though it is tempting to accept the first offer, it pays to compare the alternatives.
notes: 'lønner det seg å' — impersonal construction: it pays to

norwegian: Mange argumenterer for strengere reguleringer; _____ er andre bekymret for konsekvensene for næringslivet.
english: Many argue for stricter regulations; conversely, others are worried about the consequences for business.
notes: derimot — contrastive marker; V2: 'derimot er andre bekymret'

norwegian: Til tross for motstand fra deler av befolkningen ble vedtaket gjennomført som planlagt.
english: Despite opposition from parts of the population, the decision was implemented as planned.
notes: 'til tross for' + noun phrase; passive 'ble gjennomført' in main clause

norwegian: Hun jobber hardt og er svært kompetent; _____ har hun ikke fått den anerkjennelsen hun fortjener.
english: She works hard and is very competent; yet she has not received the recognition she deserves.
notes: likevel — 'likevel har hun ikke fått' — V2 inversion after fronted 'likevel'

norwegian: Selv om løsningen virker enkel på papiret, er den svært vanskelig å implementere i praksis.
english: Even though the solution seems simple on paper, it is very difficult to implement in practice.
notes: V2 after 'selv om'-clause; 'på papiret' — idiomatic expression

norwegian: Til tross for at de hadde ulike syn på saken, kom de til slutt frem til en felles løsning.
english: Despite having different views on the matter, they eventually reached a common solution.
notes: 'kom frem til' — phrasal verb for reaching/arriving at a conclusion

norwegian: Norsk økonomi er sterk; _____ er det store ulikheter mellom ulike regioner i landet.
english: The Norwegian economy is strong; nevertheless, there are great inequalities between different regions of the country.
notes: imidlertid — 'imidlertid er det store ulikheter' — formal contrastive, V2

norwegian: Selv om rapporten inneholdt mange gode punkter, ble den kritisert for å mangle konkrete tiltak.
english: Even though the report contained many good points, it was criticised for lacking concrete measures.
notes: Passive 'ble kritisert for å' in main clause after 'selv om'

norwegian: Tiltaket har skapt mange arbeidsplasser; _____ har det også ført til økt forurensning.
english: The measure has created many jobs; on the other hand, it has also led to increased pollution.
notes: derimot — 'derimot har det også ført til' — contrastive V2

norwegian: Til tross for at hun var den mest erfarne kandidaten, fikk hun ikke jobben.
english: Despite being the most experienced candidate, she did not get the job.
notes: 'til tross for at' + subordinate; inversion in main clause; negation position

norwegian: Selv om bevisene er begrenset, er det _____ grunn til å ta problemet på alvor.
english: Even though the evidence is limited, there is still reason to take the problem seriously.
notes: likevel — 'er det likevel grunn til' — adverb position after finite verb

norwegian: Argumentene for tiltaket er overbevisende; _____ er det knyttet stor usikkerhet til finansieringen.
english: The arguments for the measure are convincing; however, there is great uncertainty surrounding the financing.
notes: imidlertid — fronted: 'imidlertid er det knyttet' — formal V2 inversion

norwegian: Til tross for den strenge vinteren klarte bøndene å berge mesteparten av avlingen.
english: Despite the harsh winter, the farmers managed to save most of the harvest.
notes: 'til tross for' + noun phrase; 'berge' used metaphorically (save/salvage)

norwegian: Selv om reisen var lang og krevende, var det verdt det å se det vakre landskapet.
english: Even though the journey was long and demanding, it was worth it to see the beautiful landscape.
notes: 'verdt det å' — cleft-like worth construction; V2 in main clause

norwegian: Selv om uenighetene var dype, bidro dialogen _____ til å bedre forholdet mellom partene.
english: Even though the disagreements were deep, the dialogue still contributed to improving relations between the parties.
notes: likevel — 'bidro dialogen likevel til' — adverb between verb and complement

norwegian: Etter den dårlige investeringen gikk hele firmaet i hundene innen et år.
english: After the bad investment, the entire company went to the dogs within a year.
notes: 'gå i hundene' — to go to the dogs / fall apart completely

norwegian: Det er ikke bare bare å lære seg et nytt språk som voksen.
english: It is no easy feat to learn a new language as an adult.
notes: 'ikke bare bare' — not just easy / no small thing (idiom of understatement)

norwegian: Endelig tok hun bladet fra munnen og sa hva hun virkelig mente om situasjonen.
english: Finally, she spoke her mind and said what she really thought about the situation.
notes: 'ta bladet fra munnen' — to speak frankly / take the leaf from one's mouth

norwegian: Han er kjent for å alltid _____ fra munnen og si akkurat det han mener.
english: He is known for always speaking his mind and saying exactly what he thinks.
notes: ta bladet — complete idiom: 'ta bladet fra munnen'

norwegian: Prosjektet begynte bra, men mot slutten gikk det fullstendig i _____ på grunn av manglende ledelse.
english: The project started well, but towards the end it completely fell apart due to lack of leadership.
notes: hundene — 'gikk det fullstendig i hundene'

norwegian: Å få hele familien til å enes om ferieplaner er ikke bare bare.
english: Getting the whole family to agree on holiday plans is no easy task.
notes: 'ikke bare bare' at end of sentence — common colloquial placement

norwegian: Det var på tide at noen tok bladet fra munnen og påpekte det åpenbare problemet.
english: It was about time someone spoke up and pointed out the obvious problem.
notes: 'det var på tide at' — it was about time that; idiom in embedded clause

norwegian: Selv om han er flink, er han ikke noe vidunderbarn — han er _____ som alle andre.
english: Even though he is talented, he is no child prodigy — he is flesh and blood like everyone else.
notes: kjøtt og blod — 'flesh and blood' idiom for being an ordinary person

norwegian: Det er ikke lett å snu en stor organisasjon — det er ikke gjort i en håndvending.
english: It is not easy to turn a large organisation around — it cannot be done overnight.
notes: 'ikke gjort i en håndvending' — not done in the blink of an eye / not overnight

norwegian: Hun vet å holde hodet kaldt selv i de mest stressende situasjonene.
english: She knows how to keep a cool head even in the most stressful situations.
notes: 'holde hodet kaldt' — to keep a cool head; 'vet å' = knows how to

norwegian: Å finne en god leilighet i Oslo til den prisen er ikke _____ — det krever både tålmodighet og flaks.
english: Finding a good apartment in Oslo at that price is no easy feat — it requires both patience and luck.
notes: bare bare — 'ikke bare bare' idiom; position at sentence end

norwegian: Han gikk rett på sak og tok bladet fra munnen uten å nøle.
english: He got straight to the point and spoke his mind without hesitating.
notes: 'gå rett på sak' — get straight to the point; two idioms in one sentence

norwegian: Det er nå engang slik at livet ikke alltid går som man vil, og det er _____ å leve med.
english: It is simply the way it is that life does not always go as one wants, and one simply has to live with it.
notes: bare bare — 'det er bare bare å leve med' — something one simply must accept

norwegian: Etter skandalen gikk selskapets rykte fullstendig i hundene.
english: After the scandal, the company's reputation went completely to the dogs.
notes: 'gå i hundene' applied to reputation/rykte — common metaphorical use

norwegian: Journalisten tok endelig bladet fra munnen og avdekket det som alle visste men ingen ville si.
english: The journalist finally spoke out and revealed what everyone knew but no one wanted to say.
notes: Idiom in context of journalism/whistleblowing; 'avdekke' = reveal/uncover

norwegian: Å bygge tillit mellom to land som har vært i konflikt er virkelig ikke _____ .
english: Building trust between two countries that have been in conflict is truly no small matter.
notes: bare bare — idiom at sentence end after infinitive subject

norwegian: Etter mange år med dårlig ledelse var det tydelig at bedriften holdt på å gå i hundene.
english: After many years of poor management, it was clear that the company was on the verge of going under.
notes: 'holde på å gå i hundene' — progressive construction with idiom

norwegian: Sjefen er kjent for alltid å holde kortene tett til brystet i forhandlinger.
english: The boss is known for always playing his cards close to his chest in negotiations.
notes: 'holde kortene tett til brystet' — to keep one's cards close to one's chest

norwegian: Du skal ikke selge skinnet før bjørnen er skutt — vent til kontrakten er signert.
english: Don't count your chickens before they hatch — wait until the contract is signed.
notes: 'ikke selge skinnet før bjørnen er skutt' — don't sell the hide before the bear is shot

norwegian: Han _____ kortene tett til brystet under hele forhandlingsrunden.
english: He kept his cards close to his chest throughout the entire round of negotiations.
notes: holdt — 'holdt kortene tett til brystet'; past tense of 'holde'

norwegian: Det er ikke gjort i en håndvending å endre folks vaner, selv om man ønsker det.
english: Changing people's habits is not done in the blink of an eye, even if one wishes it.
notes: 'ikke gjort i en håndvending' — not done instantly; fronted for emphasis

norwegian: Hun kastet seg ut i det uten å tenke seg om — og det gikk som det pleier å gå.
english: She threw herself into it without thinking — and it went the way it usually does.
notes: 'kaste seg ut i det' — to throw oneself into something; 'gå som det pleier' — go the usual way

norwegian: Ikke _____ bjørnens skinn før bjørnen er skutt — vi vet ikke om vi får tilbudet.
english: Don't count your chickens before they hatch — we don't know if we'll get the offer.
notes: selg — imperative of 'selge'; 'ikke selg skinnet/bjørnens skinn ...'

norwegian: Det er lett å snakke om endringer, men det er ikke bare bare å gjennomføre dem.
english: It is easy to talk about changes, but it is no easy matter to carry them out.
notes: Contrast structure with 'ikke bare bare' as predicate; 'gjennomføre' = carry out

norwegian: Etter at han endelig tok bladet fra munnen, forstod alle at han hadde vært misfornøyd lenge.
english: After he finally spoke his mind, everyone understood that he had been dissatisfied for a long time.
notes: Idiom in temporal subordinate clause; pluperfect 'hadde vært' in reported state

norwegian: Nå som prosjektlederen har sluttet, er det fare for at hele satsingen går _____ .
english: Now that the project manager has left, there is a risk that the entire initiative will go under.
notes: i hundene — complete idiom: 'går i hundene'; informal prediction of failure

norwegian: Han er ingen som holder tilbake meningene sine — han tar alltid bladet fra munnen.
english: He is not someone who holds back his opinions — he always speaks his mind.
notes: 'holde tilbake meningene sine' — hold back one's opinions; contrasted with idiom

norwegian: Det er ikke gjort i en _____ å lære seg norsk til et flytende nivå.
english: Learning Norwegian to a fluent level is not done in the blink of an eye.
notes: håndvending — 'ikke gjort i en håndvending'

norwegian: Selv om forretningene gikk dårlig, nektet han å innse at firmaet holdt på å gå i hundene.
english: Even though business was going badly, he refused to accept that the company was going under.
notes: 'nekte å innse' — refuse to acknowledge; idiom with progressive 'holdt på å'

norwegian: Det er virkelig ikke bare bare å jobbe som sykepleier — det krever enorm styrke og tålmodighet.
english: It is truly no easy task to work as a nurse — it requires enormous strength and patience.
notes: Idiom with intensifier 'virkelig'; applied to a demanding profession

norwegian: Det var hun som reddet hele prosjektet i siste liten.
english: It was she who saved the entire project at the last minute.
notes: Cleft construction: 'det var ... som' — it was ... who/that

norwegian: Sjelden har jeg opplevd en så varm mottakelse som den vi fikk i Bergen.
english: Rarely have I experienced such a warm reception as the one we received in Bergen.
notes: Negative adverb fronting: 'sjelden' triggers subject-verb inversion

norwegian: Det var nettopp denne muligheten _____ hadde ventet på i årevis.
english: It was precisely this opportunity that he had been waiting for for years.
notes: han — cleft: 'det var nettopp denne muligheten han hadde ventet på'

norwegian: Aldri tidligere har norsk økonomi vokst så raskt over en så kort periode.
english: Never before has the Norwegian economy grown so rapidly over such a short period.
notes: 'aldri tidligere' fronted — triggers inversion: 'har norsk økonomi vokst'

norwegian: Det er nettopp denne forskjellen _____ gjør norsk grammatikk utfordrende for engelsktalende.
english: It is precisely this difference that makes Norwegian grammar challenging for English speakers.
notes: som — cleft relative pronoun: 'det er ... som gjør'

norwegian: Heller ikke hun visste hva som hadde skjedd med den savnede mannen.
english: She too did not know what had happened to the missing man.
notes: 'heller ikke' fronted — inversion: 'heller ikke hun visste'

norwegian: Det var i Oslo at de fleste viktige beslutningene ble tatt under krigen.
english: It was in Oslo that most of the important decisions were taken during the war.
notes: Cleft with place fronting: 'det var i Oslo at'

norwegian: Knapt hadde hun kommet hjem før telefonen begynte å ringe.
english: She had barely arrived home before the phone started ringing.
notes: 'knapt hadde hun kommet' — inversion after negative adverb 'knapt'

norwegian: Det var ikke penger _____ motiverte ham, men ønsket om å gjøre en forskjell.
english: It was not money that motivated him, but the desire to make a difference.
notes: som — negated cleft: 'det var ikke penger som motiverte ham'

norwegian: Neppe ville noen ha trodd at en slik situasjon var mulig for bare ti år siden.
english: Hardly would anyone have believed that such a situation was possible just ten years ago.
notes: 'neppe' fronted — inversion: 'neppe ville noen ha trodd'

norwegian: Det var etter denne hendelsen _____ alt forandret seg for familien.
english: It was after this event that everything changed for the family.
notes: at — cleft with time expression: 'det var etter denne hendelsen at'

norwegian: Ikke bare er det dyrt å bo i Oslo, men det er også vanskelig å finne en leilighet.
english: Not only is it expensive to live in Oslo, but it is also difficult to find an apartment.
notes: 'ikke bare ... men også' — correlative construction with inversion after 'ikke bare'

norwegian: Det var denne lærerens entusiasme _____ inspirerte ham til å studere videre.
english: It was this teacher's enthusiasm that inspired him to continue his studies.
notes: som — cleft relative pronoun

norwegian: Først når vi forstår problemets omfang, kan vi begynne å arbeide mot en løsning.
english: Only when we understand the scope of the problem can we begin to work towards a solution.
notes: 'først når' fronted temporal clause — inversion in main clause: 'kan vi begynne'

norwegian: Det var til Norge hun valgte å flytte etter mange års arbeid i utlandet.
english: It was to Norway that she chose to move after many years of working abroad.
notes: Cleft with destination: 'det var til Norge ... at/hun valgte'

norwegian: Verken tid eller penger _____ manglet på dette prosjektet — det var viljen som sviktet.
english: Neither time nor money was lacking on this project — it was the will that failed.
notes: manglet — 'verken ... eller' fronted subject triggers normal V2

norwegian: Aldri hadde han sett noe så vakkert som det norske fjordlandskapet om høsten.
english: Never had he seen anything as beautiful as the Norwegian fjord landscape in autumn.
notes: 'aldri hadde han sett' — negative adverb fronting with inversion

norwegian: Det er akkurat dette poenget _____ gjør diskusjonen så viktig og relevant.
english: It is exactly this point that makes the discussion so important and relevant.
notes: som — cleft: 'det er akkurat dette poenget som gjør'

norwegian: Ikke minst er det viktig å ivareta de ansattes trivsel i en omstillingsprosess.
english: Not least, it is important to safeguard employee well-being during a restructuring process.
notes: 'ikke minst' fronted — inversion: 'ikke minst er det viktig'

norwegian: Det var hennes ro og tålmodighet _____ gjorde henne til en utmerket leder.
english: It was her calm and patience that made her an excellent leader.
notes: som — cleft relative pronoun after compound subject

norwegian: Bare ved å lytte aktivt til hverandre kan man bygge ekte tillit i et team.
english: Only by actively listening to each other can one build genuine trust in a team.
notes: 'bare ved å' fronted — inversion: 'bare ved å lytte ... kan man bygge'

norwegian: Det er i disse øyeblikkene at man virkelig setter pris på vennskap.
english: It is in these moments that one truly appreciates friendship.
notes: Cleft with place/time: 'det er i disse øyeblikkene at'

norwegian: Ikke én gang hadde hun vurdert å gi opp drømmen om å bli lege.
english: Not once had she considered giving up her dream of becoming a doctor.
notes: 'ikke én gang' fronted — inversion: 'ikke én gang hadde hun vurdert'

norwegian: Det var under denne konferansen _____ de to forskerne møttes for første gang.
english: It was during this conference that the two researchers met for the first time.
notes: at — cleft with time/place: 'det var under denne konferansen at'

norwegian: Først da resultatene kom inn, skjønte de alvoret i situasjonen.
english: Only when the results came in did they understand the seriousness of the situation.
notes: 'først da' fronted — inversion in main clause

norwegian: Det er nettopp i slike krevende situasjoner _____ gode ledere skiller seg fra middelmådige.
english: It is precisely in such demanding situations that good leaders distinguish themselves from mediocre ones.
notes: at — cleft construction: 'det er nettopp i ... at'

norwegian: Sjelden ser man en slik kombinasjon av teknisk dyktighet og kreativitet hos en ung forsker.
english: Rarely does one see such a combination of technical skill and creativity in a young researcher.
notes: 'sjelden' fronted — inversion: 'sjelden ser man'

norwegian: Det var hans tidligere erfaringer fra utlandet _____ ga ham et unikt perspektiv på problemet.
english: It was his earlier experiences from abroad that gave him a unique perspective on the problem.
notes: Cleft with long subject: 'det var hans tidligere erfaringer ... som/at'

norwegian: Ingenlunde er det sikkert at den nye strategien vil gi de ønskede resultatene.
english: By no means is it certain that the new strategy will yield the desired results.
notes: 'ingenlunde' (by no means) fronted — formal inversion

norwegian: Det er ikke bare fagkunnskapen, men også evnen til å kommunisere, _____ arbeidsgiverne verdsetter mest.
english: It is not just subject knowledge, but also the ability to communicate, that employers value most.
notes: som — cleft with coordinated subject: 'det er ikke bare X men også Y som'

norwegian: Hun sa at hun hadde vært syk hele uken og derfor ikke kunne komme på møtet.
english: She said that she had been ill all week and therefore could not come to the meeting.
notes: Backshift: 'er syk' → 'hadde vært syk'; modal backshift: 'kan ikke' → 'ikke kunne'

norwegian: De fortalte at de ville reise til Japan neste sommer hvis de hadde råd.
english: They said that they would travel to Japan the following summer if they could afford it.
notes: Backshift: 'vil reise' → 'ville reise'; conditional in embedded clause

norwegian: Legen informerte pasienten om at testresultatene _____ klare innen to dager.
english: The doctor informed the patient that the test results would be ready within two days.
notes: ville være — backshift of future: 'er klare' → 'ville være klare'

norwegian: Han hevdet at han ikke hadde sett dokumentet og ikke visste hvem som hadde skrevet det.
english: He claimed that he had not seen the document and did not know who had written it.
notes: 'hevdet at' — speech act verb 'claim'; double backshift in coordinated clauses

norwegian: Ministeren erkjente at regjeringen _____ for sakte i saken.
english: The minister acknowledged that the government had moved too slowly in the matter.
notes: hadde beveget seg — backshift of past perfect in reported speech

norwegian: Vitnet forklarte at han hadde hørt et smell og deretter sett en person løpe ut av bygningen.
english: The witness explained that he had heard a bang and then seen a person running out of the building.
notes: Narrative reported speech with sequence of past perfect events

norwegian: Professoren understreket at studentene måtte _____ kildene sine nøye.
english: The professor emphasised that students had to check their sources carefully.
notes: sjekke — modal backshift: 'må sjekke' → 'måtte sjekke'

norwegian: Hun innrømte at hun hadde gjort en feil, men forsikret om at det ikke ville skje igjen.
english: She admitted that she had made a mistake, but assured that it would not happen again.
notes: 'innrømte at' + 'forsikret om at' — two speech act verbs; backshift in both clauses

norwegian: Sjefen bemerket at salget _____ sterkt det siste kvartalet og at de måtte finne en løsning.
english: The boss noted that sales had fallen sharply in the last quarter and that they had to find a solution.
notes: hadde falt — past perfect backshift; coordinated reported clauses

norwegian: Naboen klaget over at musikken _____ for høyt og ba dem om å skru ned volumet.
english: The neighbour complained that the music was being played too loudly and asked them to turn down the volume.
notes: ble spilt — backshift of passive present: 'spilles' → 'ble spilt'

norwegian: Eksperten advarte om at klimaendringene ville få alvorlige konsekvenser dersom ingen tiltak ble iverksatt.
english: The expert warned that climate change would have serious consequences if no measures were implemented.
notes: 'advarte om at' — speech act verb 'warn'; conditional clause with passive backshift

norwegian: De rapporterte at det hadde vært en rekke tekniske problemer, men at disse nå _____ løst.
english: They reported that there had been a number of technical problems, but that these had now been resolved.
notes: var — stative passive in backshifted clause: 'er løst' → 'var løst'

norwegian: Læreren spurte elevene om de hadde forstått oppgaven og om de trengte mer tid.
english: The teacher asked the students whether they had understood the task and whether they needed more time.
notes: Reported question with 'om' (whether); backshift in both embedded questions

norwegian: Han lovet at han _____ tilbake med svar innen fredag.
english: He promised that he would come back with an answer by Friday.
notes: ville komme — future backshift: 'kommer' → 'ville komme'

norwegian: Direktøren kunngjorde at selskapet _____ slå seg sammen med sin største konkurrent.
english: The director announced that the company would merge with its largest competitor.
notes: ville — backshift of future modal: 'skal' → 'ville'

norwegian: Forsøkspersonene rapporterte at de hadde følt seg mer avslappet etter meditasjonsøktene.
english: The test subjects reported that they had felt more relaxed after the meditation sessions.
notes: Past perfect backshift: 'følte seg' → 'hadde følt seg'

norwegian: Politiet bekreftet at de hadde pågrepet en mistenkt, men at de foreløpig ikke _____ noen tiltalte.
english: The police confirmed that they had arrested a suspect, but that they had not yet charged anyone.
notes: hadde — past perfect in negated backshifted clause

norwegian: Selskapets talsmann sa at de ikke kunne kommentere saken da undersøkelsene ennå ikke var avsluttet.
english: The company spokesperson said that they could not comment on the matter as the investigations had not yet been concluded.
notes: Modal backshift 'kan ikke' → 'ikke kunne'; causal 'da'-clause in reported speech

norwegian: Hun husket at legen hadde sagt at hun _____ unngå anstrengende aktivitet i to uker.
english: She remembered that the doctor had said that she should avoid strenuous activity for two weeks.
notes: burde — double backshift: 'bør' → 'burde'; embedded reported speech within reported speech

norwegian: Assistenten fortalte at hun hadde sendt rapporten, men at den hadde kommet i retur.
english: The assistant said that she had sent the report, but that it had come back.
notes: Past perfect backshift in coordinated reported clauses

norwegian: Undersøkelsen viste at de fleste respondentene mente at helsevesenet _____ forbedres betydelig.
english: The survey showed that most respondents felt that the healthcare system needed to be significantly improved.
notes: måtte — modal backshift: 'må' → 'måtte'; triple embedding (showed > felt > needed)

norwegian: Statsministeren uttalte at regjeringen hadde gjort alt som sto i dens makt for å håndtere krisen.
english: The Prime Minister stated that the government had done everything in its power to handle the crisis.
notes: 'uttalte at' — formal speech act verb; 'alt som sto i dens makt' — idiomatic

norwegian: Hun innrømmet at hun _____ kjent til problemet lenge, men ikke sagt noe av frykt for konsekvensene.
english: She admitted that she had known about the problem for a long time, but had not said anything out of fear of the consequences.
notes: hadde — past perfect backshift; coordinated past perfect clauses

norwegian: Journalisten spurte om statsråden visste at rapporten hadde blitt holdt tilbake i flere måneder.
english: The journalist asked whether the minister knew that the report had been withheld for several months.
notes: Reported question with 'om'; past perfect passive in embedded clause

norwegian: Kontrakten spesifiserte at alle betalinger _____ gjennomføres innen 30 dager etter fakturadato.
english: The contract specified that all payments were to be made within 30 days of the invoice date.
notes: skulle — backshift of obligation modal: 'skal' → 'skulle'

norwegian: De bemerket at det hadde tatt mye lengre tid enn de hadde forventet, og beklaget forsinkelsen.
english: They noted that it had taken much longer than they had expected, and apologised for the delay.
notes: Double past perfect; comparative backshift: 'tar' → 'hadde tatt'

norwegian: Forskeren konkluderte med at hypotesen _____ bekreftet av dataene og at videre forskning var nødvendig.
english: The researcher concluded that the hypothesis had been confirmed by the data and that further research was necessary.
notes: var blitt — past perfect passive backshift: 'er bekreftet' → 'var blitt bekreftet'

norwegian: Mannen forklarte at han ikke hadde ment å si noe støtende og bad om unnskyldning.
english: The man explained that he had not meant to say anything offensive and asked for forgiveness.
notes: Negative past perfect backshift; 'be om unnskyldning' — apologise

norwegian: Rapporten antydet at prisene _____ stige ytterligere i løpet av de neste månedene.
english: The report suggested that prices would rise further over the coming months.
notes: ville — future backshift: 'vil stige' → 'ville stige'

norwegian: Han erkjente at han hadde tatt feil og at han burde ha lyttet til rådene han fikk.
english: He acknowledged that he had been wrong and that he should have listened to the advice he was given.
notes: 'erkjente at' — acknowledge; 'burde ha lyttet' — modal perfect in backshifted clause

norwegian: Om bare jeg hadde visst det tidligere, ville jeg ha handlet helt annerledes.
english: If only I had known it earlier, I would have acted completely differently.
notes: Counterfactual: 'om bare' + past perfect + 'ville ha' + past participle

norwegian: Hvis jeg var deg, ville jeg snakket med legen så snart som mulig.
english: If I were you, I would talk to the doctor as soon as possible.
notes: Counterfactual: 'hvis jeg var deg' (Norwegian uses past tense, not a distinct subjunctive)

norwegian: Om bare hun _____ søkt om jobben tidligere, hadde hun trolig fått den.
english: If only she had applied for the job earlier, she would probably have got it.
notes: hadde — 'om bare hun hadde søkt' — past perfect in counterfactual

norwegian: Hvis han hadde tatt utdanning innen teknologi, ville han nå hatt mange flere jobbmuligheter.
english: If he had studied technology, he would now have many more job opportunities.
notes: Mixed counterfactual: past perfect condition → present result with 'nå'

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske at jeg _____ flytte til et varmere land.
english: I wish that I could move to a warmer country.
notes: kunne — 'jeg skulle ønske at jeg kunne' — wish + modal subjunctive equivalent

norwegian: Om bare det ikke hadde regnet den dagen, ville vi ha kunnet gjennomføre konserten utendørs.
english: If only it had not rained that day, we would have been able to hold the concert outdoors.
notes: Negated past perfect counterfactual; 'ville ha kunnet' — modal in result clause

norwegian: Hadde de _____ bedre forberedt, ville utfallet trolig blitt annerledes.
english: Had they been better prepared, the outcome would probably have been different.
notes: vært — inverted counterfactual without 'hvis/om': 'hadde de vært'

norwegian: Hvis verden hadde reagert raskere, kunne klimakrisen vært unngått.
english: If the world had reacted faster, the climate crisis could have been avoided.
notes: Counterfactual with passive result clause: 'kunne vært unngått'

norwegian: Om jeg bare hadde lest kontrakten grundigere, _____ jeg ikke havnet i denne situasjonen.
english: If only I had read the contract more thoroughly, I would not have ended up in this situation.
notes: ville — 'ville jeg ikke havnet' — result clause in counterfactual

norwegian: Hadde vi hatt mer tid, kunne vi ha gjort en grundigere analyse av dataene.
english: Had we had more time, we could have done a more thorough analysis of the data.
notes: Inverted counterfactual: 'hadde vi hatt' — no 'hvis/om'

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske at han hadde _____ ærligere mot meg fra begynnelsen av.
english: I wish that he had been more honest with me from the beginning.
notes: vært — past perfect in wish construction: 'hadde vært ærligere'

norwegian: Hvis teknologien hadde eksistert på den tiden, ville livet sett veldig annerledes ut.
english: If the technology had existed at that time, life would have looked very different.
notes: 'ville sett ut' — result clause with directional particle; past perfect condition

norwegian: Om bare jeg _____ mer penger, ville jeg reist verden rundt.
english: If only I had more money, I would travel around the world.
notes: hadde — present counterfactual using past tense: 'om bare jeg hadde'

norwegian: Hadde han visst hva som ventet, ville han aldri gått med på avtalen.
english: Had he known what was waiting, he would never have agreed to the deal.
notes: Inverted counterfactual; negation 'aldri' in result clause with inversion

norwegian: Hvis jeg hadde valgt en annen karrierevei, ville livet mitt ha sett veldig annerledes ut.
english: If I had chosen a different career path, my life would have looked very different.

norwegian: Om bare hun hadde takket nei til tilbudet, kunne alt ha vært unngått.
english: If only she had declined the offer, everything could have been avoided.
notes: 'takke nei til' — turn down/decline; passive counterfactual result

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske at vi _____ kjent hverandre bedre da vi var unge.
english: I wish that we had known each other better when we were young.
notes: hadde

norwegian: Hvis hun hadde lyttet til foreldrenes råd, ville hun sluppet mange unødvendige problemer.
english: If she had listened to her parents' advice, she would have avoided many unnecessary problems.
notes: 'slippe' in result clause — avoid/escape; past perfect condition

norwegian: Hadde prosjektet _____ fullfinansiert fra starten, hadde vi kunnet levere resultater mye raskere.
english: Had the project been fully funded from the start, we could have delivered results much faster.
notes: vært — inverted counterfactual with passive: 'hadde prosjektet vært fullfinansiert'

norwegian: Hvis klimatiltakene hadde blitt iverksatt på 1990-tallet, ville situasjonen i dag sett mye bedre ut.
english: If climate measures had been implemented in the 1990s, the situation today would look much better.
notes: Passive past perfect condition; mixed result with present time reference 'i dag'

norwegian: Om bare han hadde _____ litt mer tålmodighet, ville forhandlingene ha gått mye bedre.
english: If only he had had a little more patience, the negotiations would have gone much better.
notes: hatt — past perfect of 'ha': 'hadde hatt litt mer tålmodighet'

norwegian: Hadde forskerne hatt bedre utstyr, kunne de ha gjort viktige oppdagelser mye tidligere.
english: Had the researchers had better equipment, they could have made important discoveries much earlier.
notes: Inverted counterfactual; 'kunne ha gjort' — modal perfect in result clause

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske det fantes en enklere løsning, men dessverre er virkeligheten mer kompleks.
english: I wish there were a simpler solution, but unfortunately reality is more complex.
notes: 'jeg skulle ønske det fantes' — wish construction with past tense 'fantes'

norwegian: Hvis de hadde investert i fornybar energi tidligere, _____ de nå spart store summer.
english: If they had invested in renewable energy earlier, they would now have saved large sums.
notes: ville — 'ville de nå spart' — mixed counterfactual with present adverb 'nå'

norwegian: Om bare menneskene hadde forstått konsekvensene, ville mange katastrofer ha kunnet unngås.
english: If only people had understood the consequences, many disasters could have been avoided.
notes: Passive modal counterfactual result: 'ville ha kunnet unngås'

norwegian: Hadde du _____ meg om problemet, hadde jeg hjulpet deg med en gang.
english: Had you told me about the problem, I would have helped you straight away.
notes: fortalt — inverted counterfactual: 'hadde du fortalt meg'

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske at hun fortsatt var her — det er så mye jeg gjerne ville ha sagt henne.
english: I wish she were still here — there is so much I would have liked to say to her.
notes: Wish construction; 'ville ha sagt' — modal perfect expressing unfulfilled desire

norwegian: Hvis man hadde satset mer på forebygging, _____ helsevesenet spart store ressurser.
english: If more had been invested in prevention, the healthcare system would have saved large resources.
notes: ville — result clause: 'ville helsevesenet spart' — inversion in conditional result

norwegian: Hadde de to landene samarbeidet bedre, kunne konflikten vært unngått.
english: Had the two countries cooperated better, the conflict could have been avoided.
notes: Inverted counterfactual; passive modal result: 'kunne vært unngått'

norwegian: Om bare teknologien hadde vært mer tilgjengelig da, ville mange flere hatt tilgang til utdanning.
english: If only the technology had been more accessible then, many more would have had access to education.
notes: Past counterfactual with temporal 'da'; 'ville mange flere hatt' — inversion in result

norwegian: Rapporten påpekte flere alvorlige mangler. Til tross for dette valgte ledelsen å ikke iverksette tiltak.
english: The report pointed out several serious deficiencies. Despite this, management chose not to implement measures.
notes: Anaphoric: 'til tross for dette' refers back to the reported deficiencies

norwegian: Som nevnt tidligere, er det viktig å skille mellom kortsiktige og langsiktige løsninger.
english: As mentioned earlier, it is important to distinguish between short-term and long-term solutions.
notes: 'som nevnt tidligere' — anaphoric reference to prior text; V2 inversion

norwegian: Metoden ble beskrevet i kapittel to. _____ er det nødvendig å forstå denne metoden godt.
english: The method was described in chapter two. For this reason, it is necessary to understand this method well.
notes: Av denne grunn — causal connector; V2 inversion: 'av denne grunn er det'

norwegian: Problemet er komplekst og krever en helhetlig tilnærming. I tillegg må man ta hensyn til lokale forhold.
english: The problem is complex and requires a holistic approach. In addition, one must take local conditions into account.
notes: 'i tillegg' — additive cohesive connector; V2 inversion

norwegian: Undersøkelsen viste positive resultater. _____ bør man ikke trekke for raske konklusjoner.
english: The survey showed positive results. Nevertheless, one should not draw hasty conclusions.
notes: Ikke desto mindre/Likevel — concessive connector; V2: 'likevel bør man'

norwegian: Det første alternativet er billigere, men det andre er mer bærekraftig. Sistnevnte er derfor å foretrekke.
english: The first alternative is cheaper, but the second is more sustainable. The latter is therefore preferable.
notes: 'sistnevnte' — anaphoric reference (the latter); 'å foretrekke' — infinitive as predicate

norwegian: Regjeringen presenterte planen sin i januar. _____ er den blitt møtt med blandet mottakelse.
english: The government presented its plan in January. Since then, it has been met with a mixed reception.
notes: Siden da — temporal cohesive connector; V2 inversion: 'siden da er den blitt'

norwegian: Tiltaket hadde god effekt i det første halvåret. Imidlertid begynte resultatene å avta mot slutten av året.
english: The measure had a good effect in the first half of the year. However, the results began to decline towards the end of the year.
notes: 'imidlertid' — formal contrastive connector between sentences; V2

norwegian: Forsøket mislyktes i første omgang. _____ ga ikke teamet opp og prøvde på nytt med en justert metode.
english: The experiment failed on the first attempt. Nevertheless, the team did not give up and tried again with an adjusted method.
notes: Ikke desto mindre — formal concessive connector; V2 inversion

norwegian: Det er to mulige løsninger. Førstnevnte krever mer ressurser, men sistnevnte tar lengre tid å implementere.
english: There are two possible solutions. The former requires more resources, but the latter takes longer to implement.
notes: 'førstnevnte/sistnevnte' — the former/latter; anaphoric pair reference

norwegian: Resultatene fra den første studien er lovende. _____ er det for tidlig å trekke endelige konklusjoner.
english: The results from the first study are promising. Even so, it is too early to draw final conclusions.
notes: Likevel — concessive inter-sentence connector; V2: 'likevel er det'

norwegian: Vi analyserte tre ulike tilnærminger. Den første viste seg å være mest effektiv under de gitte forholdene.
english: We analysed three different approaches. The first proved to be most effective under the given conditions.
notes: 'den første' — anaphoric ordinal reference back to 'tre ulike tilnærminger'

norwegian: Prosjektet ble ferdigstilt i tide og innenfor budsjett. Dessuten overgikk det forventningene til alle involverte parter.
english: The project was completed on time and within budget. Moreover, it exceeded the expectations of all involved parties.
notes: 'dessuten' — additive connector (moreover); V2 inversion

norwegian: Den nye loven trådte i kraft 1. januar. _____ har det vært stor debatt om dens praktiske konsekvenser.
english: The new law came into force on 1 January. Since then, there has been much debate about its practical consequences.
notes: Siden da — temporal cohesive connector; V2: 'siden da har det vært'

norwegian: Deltakerne ble delt inn i to grupper. Den ene gruppen fikk behandling, mens den andre fungerte som kontrollgruppe.
english: The participants were divided into two groups. One group received treatment, while the other served as a control group.
notes: 'den ene ... den andre' — cohesive pair reference; 'mens' = contrastive subordinator

norwegian: Selskapet ble grunnlagt i 2005 og vokste raskt. _____ slet det med likviditeten i 2018.
english: The company was founded in 2005 and grew rapidly. However, it struggled with liquidity in 2018.
notes: Imidlertid — formal contrastive connector; V2: 'imidlertid slet det'

norwegian: Klimaforskerne har advart om konsekvensene i årevis. _____ har politikerne ikke handlet raskt nok.
english: Climate scientists have been warning about the consequences for years. Despite this, politicians have not acted quickly enough.
notes: Til tross for dette — concessive anaphoric connector; V2 inversion

norwegian: Det ble presentert to forslag på møtet. Begge hadde sine fordeler, men styret valgte likevel det første.
english: Two proposals were presented at the meeting. Both had their advantages, but the board nonetheless chose the first.
notes: 'begge' — anaphoric quantifier; 'det første' — anaphoric ordinal reference

norwegian: Prisen på råolje steg kraftig i fjerde kvartal. _____ ser markedet ut til å stabilisere seg.
english: The price of crude oil rose sharply in the fourth quarter. Now, the market appears to be stabilising.
notes: Nå — temporal cohesive adverb; V2: 'nå ser markedet ut til'

norwegian: Prosjektet hadde tre hovedmål. Det første ble nådd, det andre delvis, og det tredje ikke i det hele tatt.
english: The project had three main goals. The first was achieved, the second partially, and the third not at all.
notes: Anaphoric ordinals 'det første/andre/tredje'; ellipsis in coordinated clauses

norwegian: Regjeringen innførte nye avgifter på flyreiser. _____ har antallet reiser gått ned med ti prosent.
english: The government introduced new taxes on air travel. As a result, the number of journeys has fallen by ten per cent.
notes: Som følge av dette — causal cohesive connector; V2: 'som følge av dette har antallet'

norwegian: Artikkelen beskriver to teorier. Disse er henholdsvis kjent som evolusjonsteori og skapelsesteori.
english: The article describes two theories. These are respectively known as evolution theory and creation theory.
notes: 'disse' — anaphoric plural pronoun; 'henholdsvis' — respectively (formal cohesive)

norwegian: Forhandlingene brøt sammen i mai. _____ kom partene tilbake til bordet i september.
english: The negotiations broke down in May. Nevertheless, the parties came back to the table in September.
notes: Ikke desto mindre — formal concessive connector; V2 inversion

norwegian: Analysen viste tre risikofaktorer. Den viktigste av disse er mangelen på tilstrekkelig finansiering.
english: The analysis revealed three risk factors. The most important of these is the lack of adequate financing.
notes: 'den viktigste av disse' — anaphoric superlative reference back to previous sentence

norwegian: Selskapet investerte tungt i ny teknologi. Det viste seg _____ å være en dårlig beslutning.
english: The company invested heavily in new technology. This turned out, however, to be a poor decision.
notes: imidlertid — parenthetical mid-clause: 'viste seg imidlertid å være'

norwegian: Tre kandidater ble innkalt til intervju. Av disse ble til slutt kun én tilbudt stillingen.
english: Three candidates were called for interview. Of these, ultimately only one was offered the position.
notes: 'av disse' — partitive anaphoric reference; V2 after fronted 'av disse'

norwegian: Den første løsningen er rask, men kostbar. _____ er den andre løsningen billigere, men tar mer tid.
english: The first solution is quick but costly. Conversely, the second solution is cheaper but takes more time.
notes: Derimot — contrastive connector; V2: 'derimot er den andre løsningen'

norwegian: Arbeidet med rapporten tok fire måneder. _____ ble den godt mottatt av alle interessenter.
english: The work on the report took four months. In the end, it was well received by all stakeholders.
notes: Til slutt — temporal-sequential connector; V2: 'til slutt ble den'

norwegian: Forskningsmiljøet er delt i synet på dette. Noen mener at klimaendringene er menneskeskapte, mens andre hevder det motsatte.
english: The research community is divided on this. Some believe that climate change is man-made, while others claim the opposite.
notes: 'dette' — anaphoric pronoun; 'noen ... andre' — contrastive pair; 'det motsatte' — anaphoric noun

norwegian: Rådet anbefalte tre tiltak. Det siste av disse vil imidlertid kreve betydelige investeringer.
english: The council recommended three measures. The last of these will, however, require significant investments.
notes: 'det siste av disse' — anaphoric ordinal partitive reference; 'imidlertid' parenthetical

norwegian: Den syngende fuglen vekket henne tidlig om morgenen.
english: The singing bird woke her early in the morning.
notes: Present participle as attributive adjective: 'syngende' modifies 'fuglen'

norwegian: Et velskrevet brev kan åpne mange dører i yrkeslivet.
english: A well-written letter can open many doors in professional life.
notes: Past participle as attributive adjective: 'velskrevet' modifies 'brev'

norwegian: Den _____ mannen satt alene på benken og stirret ut i luften.
english: The grieving man sat alone on the bench and stared into the air.
notes: sørgende — present participle as adjective: from 'å sørge' (to grieve)

norwegian: Rapporten inneholder mange overraskende funn som utfordrer gjeldende teorier.
english: The report contains many surprising findings that challenge existing theories.
notes: 'overraskende' — present participle as adjective; 'gjeldende' — present participle meaning current/applicable

norwegian: Det _____ barnet ble funnet trygt og uskadelig etter en times søk.
english: The missing child was found safe and unharmed after an hour of searching.
notes: savnede — past participle as adjective (definite form): from 'å savne'

norwegian: En godt forberedt presentasjon gjør alltid et sterkt inntrykk på publikum.
english: A well-prepared presentation always makes a strong impression on an audience.
notes: 'godt forberedt' — adverb + past participle as compound attributive adjective

norwegian: De _____ turister spurte oss om veien til hotellet.
english: The lost tourists asked us for directions to the hotel.
notes: fortapte/bortkomne — past participle as adjective (plural definite)

norwegian: Hun er kjent for sin beroligende stemme og evnen til å få folk til å slappe av.
english: She is known for her calming voice and the ability to make people relax.
notes: 'beroligende' — present participle as attributive adjective modifying 'stemme'

norwegian: De nylig _____ dataene viser en klar forbedring i pasientenes tilstand.
english: The recently collected data shows a clear improvement in the patients' condition.
notes: innsamlede — past participle as adjective (plural/definite); 'nylig innsamlede' = recently collected

norwegian: Det er et fascinerende spørsmål som filosofer har diskutert i hundrevis av år.
english: It is a fascinating question that philosophers have debated for hundreds of years.
notes: 'fascinerende' — present participle as attributive adjective

norwegian: En _____ student vil alltid finne måter å forbedre seg på.
english: A motivated student will always find ways to improve.
notes: motivert — past participle as adjective (indefinite singular common gender)

norwegian: De overlevende passasjerene ble fraktet til sykehus for behandling.
english: The surviving passengers were transported to hospital for treatment.
notes: 'overlevende' — present participle used as both adjective and noun; here as adjective

norwegian: Et velorganisert arrangement gjør det mulig for alle deltakerne å delta aktivt.
english: A well-organised event makes it possible for all participants to participate actively.
notes: 'velorganisert' — compound past participle as attributive adjective

norwegian: Den _____ forskningen på feltet har enda ikke gitt noen klare svar.
english: The ongoing research in the field has not yet provided any clear answers.
notes: pågående — present participle as attributive adjective: ongoing

norwegian: De utslitte løperne krysset mållinjen etter et langt og krevende løp.
english: The exhausted runners crossed the finish line after a long and demanding race.
notes: 'utslitte' — past participle as plural attributive adjective

norwegian: Det _____ beløpet vil bli overført til din konto innen tre virkedager.
english: The agreed amount will be transferred to your account within three working days.
notes: avtalte — past participle as definite attributive adjective: agreed/stipulated

norwegian: Den voksende befolkningen i byene skaper press på infrastrukturen.
english: The growing urban population creates pressure on the infrastructure.
notes: 'voksende' — present participle as attributive adjective

norwegian: Et godt _____ argument er mer overbevisende enn en svak og generell påstand.
english: A well-reasoned argument is more convincing than a weak and general claim.
notes: begrunnet — past participle as attributive adjective: reasoned/substantiated

norwegian: De engasjerte studentene stilte mange spørsmål etter forelesningen.
english: The engaged students asked many questions after the lecture.
notes: 'engasjerte' — past participle as plural definite attributive adjective

norwegian: Det brennende spørsmålet nå er hvem som skal ta ansvar for konsekvensene.
english: The burning question now is who is to take responsibility for the consequences.
notes: 'brennende' — present participle as attributive adjective (idiomatic: burning question)

norwegian: De _____ konsekvensene av klimaendringene er enda ikke fullt ut forstått.
english: The persistent consequences of climate change are not yet fully understood.
notes: vedvarende

norwegian: En klarsynt leder ser problemer før de oppstår og handler proaktivt.
english: A clear-sighted leader sees problems before they arise and acts proactively.
notes: 'klarsynt' — compound adjective used attributively; note V2 in relative clause

norwegian: Det _____ samarbeidet mellom de to landene har lagt grunnlaget for varig fred.
english: The sustained cooperation between the two countries has laid the foundation for lasting peace.
notes: vedvarende — present participle as attributive adjective: sustained/ongoing

norwegian: Den overbevisende talen fikk mange skeptikere til å endre mening.
english: The convincing speech made many sceptics change their minds.
notes: 'overbevisende' — present participle as attributive adjective; 'få til å' — causative construction

norwegian: Et _____ forsøk er bedre enn ingen, selv om det ikke lykkes fullt ut.
english: A failed attempt is better than none, even if it does not fully succeed.
notes: mislykket — past participle as indefinite attributive adjective

norwegian: De raskt skiftende markedsforholdene gjør det vanskelig å planlegge langsiktig.
english: The rapidly changing market conditions make it difficult to plan long-term.
notes: 'raskt skiftende' — adverb + present participle as compound attributive

norwegian: Hun hadde en avslappet holdning til livet som mange beundret.
english: She had a relaxed attitude to life that many admired.
notes: 'avslappet' — past participle as attributive adjective (indefinite singular common)
